reasoning: I will vote for Mamata's party because the CAA still does not say my name, and Shantanu Thakur—a Matua who now serves as Union Minister—is part of the government that passed it. My family has been here four generations with documents older than most Matua families, yet the law protects them and excludes me because I am Muslim. Yes, Mamata steals, and my cousin lost his teacher's job, and my uncle never got his full wages—I see this corruption with my own eyes. But Mamata is the only one who has promised no NRC in Bengal, and that promise means I do not have to lie awake wondering if next year I will have to prove my grandfather's migration date in a court. I would rather vote for a thief who lets me exist as a citizen than a government that makes me a suspect in my own village.
primary_drivers: CAA excludes Muslims while protecting Hindu/Sikh/Christian refugees, and I am still a suspect in my own land after four generations
Mamata's explicit 'no NRC' promise is the only protection against erasure I have
Cannot vote for BJP/Shantanu Thakur, the party and candidate that represent Hindu nationalist exclusion, despite genuine anger at TMC corruption
